discovery.run_summary: All six earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover-page documents — no Exhibit 99.1 financial detail was readable, severely limiting guidance-pattern reads. Strongest filing-grounded calls: ASAN (CEO/COO/GC/CFO transitions stacked with cut FY26 guide pattern reflected in 8-K disclosures = OVERDONE risk), and MOD (Reverse Morris Trust spin pending introduces fresh 10-Q risk factors and execution distraction). Others classified RATIONAL or UNCLEAR given the lack of P&L visibility in the filings bundle.

discoveries.name: Asana, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: The Nov 30, 2025 8-K disclosed simultaneous resignations of the COO (Anne Raimondi) and General Counsel, accompanied by 'updated guidance for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2026' — typically a downward revision context. The Mar 2, 2026 8-K announced an additional $160M buyback authorization, often used to cushion soft prints. The 10-K shows net loss widened-context ($189M FY26) and dollar-based net retention at only 97% for Core and 96% for $100K+ customers — both sub-100%, indicating contraction within the existing base. Q3 10-Q risk factors flag 'longer sales cycles' and 'reduction in renewal rates' as ongoing.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cover-page 8-Ks reference 'updated guidance' in both Sept 2025 and Dec 2025 prints — pattern suggests management has been resetting expectations downward each quarter through FY26.
discoveries.setup: Pre-Q1 FY27 print with NRR below 100%, stacked C-suite departures (COO, GC, plus prior CFO/CEO turnover at peer SaaS names), and a freshly enlarged buyback that reads as defensive.
discoveries.thesis: Filings show a sub-100% net retention business with repeated guidance resets and significant executive departures right before the print. The market may be under-pricing the risk that retention deteriorates further or that the new leadership team uses the print as a 'kitchen sink' opportunity.
discoveries.what_confirms: NRR ticking down again from 97%, FY27 guidance materially below current consensus, billings deceleration, or further commentary on macro/budget scrutiny in customer accounts.
discoveries.what_kills: AI Studio / AI Teammates monetization showing real ARR contribution, NRR stabilizing or upticking, or large-customer count ($100K+) re-accelerating which would suggest the enterprise motion is finally compounding.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a SaaS company has DBNRR <100% AND stacked senior departures AND raises buyback simultaneously with earnings, that combination historically precedes guide-downs more often than upside surprises.
research_pointers: Check whether Q4 FY26 (reported Mar 2026) DBNRR was 97% or lower vs. the 96-97% trend
Verify FY27 revenue guide vs. consensus on the May 28 print
Look for any 8-K between Mar and May 2026 disclosing further executive departures

discoveries.name: Modine Manufacturing Company
discoveries.filings_evidence: The Feb 5, 2026 10-Q added four entirely new risk factors related to the proposed Reverse Morris Trust spin of Performance Technologies into Gentherm — including explicit 'uncertainties while the Reverse Morris Trust transaction is pending,' employee/customer distraction risk, and the warning that 'we may be unable to achieve some or all of the anticipated benefits.' The 10-K shows FY25 revenue of $2.6B (+7% YoY) with Data Center Cooling now 25% of revenue (up from 12%), but Performance Technologies softness in agriculture and commercial vehicle markets.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cannot read guidance pattern — all four earnings 8-Ks are cover-page-only fallbacks with no financial detail.
discoveries.setup: Pre-Q4 FY26 print with a transformational spin-merger pending, Data Center Cooling momentum, and Performance Technologies headwinds.
discoveries.thesis: Filings present a genuinely two-sided setup: bullish Data Center Cooling secular tailwind vs. bearish PT softness plus deal-execution risk and management distraction. Without earnings P&L visibility, conviction is too low to commit directionally.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a mid-cap announces a Reverse Morris Trust shortly before a print, the 10-Q risk factor delta is the single most important read; track whether management distraction shows up in the print as missed PT margins.
research_pointers: Pull the actual Q3 FY26 Exhibit 99.1 to read Data Center Cooling growth rate and PT margin trajectory
Check Gentherm transaction timeline updates and any regulatory approval news
Watch backlog disclosure for Data Center Cooling in the print

skipped.reason: Earnings 8-Ks are all cover-page fallbacks — no visibility into book-to-bill, backlog or guidance pattern. 10-K flags DOGE/contract-review headwinds but no fresh Q10 risk factor changes; filings too thin for a confident directional read.

All four earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover documents. 10-K shows non-cancellable backlog grew to $1.06B from $861M (+23%) and net retention only 104% — mixed but not directionally decisive without revenue/billings detail. RATIONAL.

Earnings 8-Ks are cover-page fallbacks. 10-K notes Elastic Cloud now 46% of revenue and acknowledges 'slowing consumption' but no recent risk factor changes in Q10. Insufficient filing evidence to commit directionally — UNCLEAR.

Significant CEO transition (Ittycheria out, Desai in Nov 2025) and CFO/CAO changes disclosed in earnings 8-Ks, but Q3 print (Dec 2025) was already absorbed by the market. The Q10 risk factor specifically calls out 'slower than historical growth of our existing Atlas applications' — bearish signal — but new CEO's first full quarter may have reset bar low. Mixed signals; filing evidence too two-sided for a high-conviction directional call — UNCLEAR.

discovery.no_signals_note: The earnings 8-K bundle uniformly fell back to cover-page documents across all six candidates, removing the most important Screen 2 input (Exhibit 99.1 prepared financials and guidance commentary). This forced higher-than-usual reliance on 10-K/10-Q risk factor deltas and structural disclosures. Only ASAN had a filing-grounded directional read at confidence 3.
